days.message:
I love that I met you. Well, when we first met, I didn't really think of it as much, to be honest. Rishabh's girl. That's all you were to me really. Before I even met you at Campbell Pizza and shook your hand, I had already seen numerous photos of you from the phone of Mr. Swarankar, but I was always just like "Cool bro, hope it works out for you." Funny how today the situation has swapped completely (switcharoo?), and I bet he would have that response if I showed him pictures instead. But anyways, I didn't think of you much back then. You were just the pretty girl my friend wanted to date. Next time we met was at my housewarming, and you came with Rishabh and Sonali. That was the first time I actually talked to you, and introduced myself. "She's pretty cool and sociable," I thought. I know afterwards I ignored you and walked away, but that was because I hated myself and I'm insecure (We will get to insecurities in a later chapter :), but I did actually enjoy talking with you! I thought you were pretty as well, and not very, but slightly jealous that Rishabh had a chance to get a beautiful girlfriend like you. Also I was annoyed that Archit got your's and Sonali's numbers that night. It should have been me :((( .

At this time, I didn't really see you as a friend. Yeah she's cool, hope it works out for Rishabh, and I can't wait to make new friends in this new city I'm in. But that changed the day of Jasmeet's bday party/Santacon. I was playing drinking games at Jasmeet's and a drunk Archit and sober Rishabh pop in at like 11 pm? Archit's telling me about Manasa's friend, I forget her name and a potential date he has. Legend, btw. And Rishabh is moody? idk? and he was just asking us about you and what he should do. We were driving back from Jasmeet's to the Mission, and Rishabh said he was going to go over to your place. Aman suggested that he ask you if you wanted food and we would pick it up. Wingman, btw. He asked, you said no, and I was like "Bro who rejects Homeroom, lowkey red flag Rishabh, time to go next." We still went over to your place because you were kind enough to invite us over, but it felt so odd. Riya passing out, your place with no light. Everyone wanted to go home but Rishabh, but as his wingmen we had to pull through. I am so, so happy we did. That was the first time I actually talked to you and learned that you liked league??? You even had zyra keycaps and took us to your room to show it. I thought you were incredibly friendly, and at that point knew that I wanted to be friends with you. I think you even offered a white claw, but we were so dead and rejected it. Kind gesture tho. And then Rishabh asked you out blah blah blah, you broke his heart and all. Unforgettable night :)

Okay, end of Rishabh stuff, wow I blabber a lot. Now it's just you and me. I'm gonna skip archit's bday and how I liked how interested you were in talking to me. How you asked me to show you my Braum, and that was kind of hot tbh. And how I remember singing karaoke songs with you and Danilo at Rishabh's bday. And you were impressed that he knew Korean, and that's when I learned you were die-hard Korean stan or something. Ok enough of that, I want to talk about how I first got feelings for you to conclude this chapter. Otherwise I would be writing the whole day just about Jasmeet's bday. I always want to tell you everything, down to the minute detail.

Focus Suraj. I think my feelings of friendship started culminating for you ever since zyra keycaps day and playing league thanks to the archit bet :), but thinking back I wonder when it switched. I know I tell you it was Butter and vibing with you, and the fact that I felt so protective of you when Riya ousted you. Maybe that was all it took for me to see you romantically, but I feel like the groundwork was already laid out from our closeness in the previous months. I would still see you every now and then, and I remember I would laugh and smile, like even back to your housewarming or Kenny's. We would always just smile and laugh together. I guess that's all it takes for an initial attraction for me. Sharing the shroom chocolate really sealed the deal that day for me. You don't remember, but at the end before we left, I had half in my mouth, and you leaned into me and bit the other half off. Heart skipped a beat then. DEEPTHI, why didn't you ask me to kiss you again that day, I didn't hear you the first time :(( I then walked home, and told Archit how I think I have feelings for you now, and the story started that day.

This is just the start of your 30 days of stories, experiences, visuals, love, and more. Today was just reminiscing to be honest, but I hope we learn more about each other as the days go on. I love you.
questions: When did you first think of me as a friend?
Do you remember your emotions when you first saw me in a romantic context?
Is it cringe that I'm voicing out the entire thing?

days.message:
I love how curious you are. You always ask questions. Doesn't matter if it is an obscure topic or one that you are not knowledgeable about, you always ask questions. And it's not surface level questions for the sake of conversation, you actually dig deeper than most people. This is a trait I noticed in you pretty early on specifically with your conversations with people at parties. Do you ever wonder why people enjoy talking with you? It's because you show interest in what people do/are passionate about, and everyone loves that feeling. Not only that, you actually make people think when conversing with you. Yes, maybe some people don't like that, but I think that is one of your strongest characteristics. Also, you are VERY good at reading a room, one of the most aware people I know, so you can gauge if someone wants to dig deeper or not. One of the earliest examples I can think of is Kenny's housewarming when I was sitting on their couch kind of bored. This was after I talked with Manasa the whole night and Rishabh was trying to set me up. As I was sitting down, both you and Prashanth sat next to me and striked up a conversation. You asked me "Suraj, why are you not going for any girls like Archit? You like never seem to flirt with anyone." I still remember that vividly. I actually had to pause and think why. I was talking to a girl currently, like every couple days, but it really wasn't serious at all. Never told you this, but that conversation actually led me to re-think why I'm not trying to date/flirt when I had all the opportunities in the world. That actually made me consider dating other people actively. Just one thing your curiosity led to. I know it may seem like you didn't really do much, but no one had asked me that prior, for like the previous 4ish months. Thanks for asking me :)

Your curiosity transcends just conversations. You are so curious about your passions and what people think about it. When I told you I'm struggling with green beans, you literally read into it and sent me screenshots from THE book. I forgot the name of the book, please remind me :) Let's not get started about the running. I have never, and I probably will never find anyone else, more curious about my running career (Yes, I call it a career). Name a person who knows my 5k, 10k, and half marathon PR dates by heart. And the mile of course. I hurt myself, you will ask me how. I run at a 7:15 pace when I said I need to do a recovery run, you will ask me why. I go shopping for new shoes, you will ask me what I tried. Not only that, you would go into depth about each one, until it was a 2 hour conversation about just the first shoe. Tangents might be a major cause of this as well. Don't get me started with triangulation. But also, you being this curious, makes me think more and actually appreciate the things I do and accomplish. You make me so introspective and I love that. There's also a lot more here about how you inspire me to be curious, but keep waiting, that will come in later chapters don't worry.

I know you are a bit biased towards me, but I love the curiosity you show and it makes me excited to share stories with you because I know you care A LOT. Thank you for being so curious, and asking me even the small things. I always thought of myself as a curious person, a person who always wants to learn more, but after meeting you I realize I am just a novice. But I'm getting better! And I'm always genuinely curious about you. How was your field day? How was your week thus far? I wanted to give you a 3.5 hour summary of my meal with Aman, and get your insights on it so badly. Hopefully my brief, brief summary on insta satisfied you slightly. Yes, that was for you. I've never been more curious about anyone else in my life, and I can promise you that is not going to stop, Deepthi. I love you.
questions: Is there anything that you are curious about, but too afraid to ask?
Do you wish I would ask you more questions?
Do you consider yourself a curious person? (generally, more than just for me)

days.message:
I love how adventurous you are. I'm going to repeating this a lot over the next 27 days, but this is a characteristic of yours I really love. You always have a desire in yourself to explore and do more. I know some of your earlier hikes were Rish's idea, probably Diablo and Whitney, but not anyone would actually do those if asked. You take those risks that others wouldn't when it comes to new experiences and that has shaped you into the person you are today. I still remember the first hike we went on together, Alamere Falls. The hike was pretty lackluster imo, and the reward of that waterfall wasn't worth it, but it was the friends that really made it worth it :) It was right after you rejected my first confession so I was still stinging slightly from that, but it was fun hiking with you from start to finish. Do you remember your blister? It was so fun mocking your ridiculous sideways walk on the hike, as well as while walking to the shitty Thai restaurant which we will never bring up or suggest ever again. Who knew Pad See Ew could be even sweeter :'( Although we laughed at you, I viewed it as strength even though I didn't explicitly say it. This girl hiked Diablo in 95 degree weather, almost died, got painful blisters, and is still pulling her own weight on this hike??? Thoroughly impressed, Deepthi. Also side note, I liked staring at your butt as you walked in front of me, and holding your hand to help you up the "shortcut" on the way back :) BUT more importantly, this hike made me appreciate both hiking with friends more as well as hiking with you. I knew I could count on you to pull through on any fun adventure we go on. So I'm gonna count on you to join me if we ever go scuba diving! Also sky diving. I want to go sky diving with you really badly. It is like another extreme activity that you haven't done and I want to be the first one to do it with you. I want you to experience it with me.

I want to travel with you, Deepthi. Your "always down" attitude and curiosity really makes you the perfect travel partner. I know you would probably still spend like 2-3 days of a trip working, but otherwise I'd be so excited to explore with you. I guess you don't know what it would be like to travel with me, so let me tell you what it would be like. First, we will be running at least once in every country we visit. 5 miles minimum. It's the perfect way to explore a new location and see unique areas not listed on a tourist guide. I also like running to parks or along sea/ocean/lake fronts. You would have loved my Europe trip and all the great locations I was able to run in. Zurich was okay, but when we go to Switzerland together again (because it is so beautiful I have to show you), I want to go running around Interlaken. Imagine running through a still flat area surrounded by beautiful mountains and greenery around you. It's breathtaking Deepthi. We also have to go paragliding there, it is so popular and no one wanted to do it with me :( As my love, you can't say no and we have to do it. I'll hold onto you if you are scared of course. Ok sorry, back to what I would be like on a trip. Next, this is a new addition, but we have to look for restaurants which showcase the local cuisine well. Maybe bib gourmand or local recommendations to find a good spot. Originally, I was more of an "eat to live" kind of guy even on trips, and Aman would drag me to fancy restaurants. I still don't care too much about going to fancy restaurants, but I'm curious to try local foods. Like bro, what is a Tajin, I want to try that. Juices are popular here? I want to try them then. I think I've been lacking the food experience on my trips, but you make me want to try them more. Well you were the inspiration, now I just want to try them for my own sake. I like food. I made us all try Surinamese food in Amsterdam because it was popular local food and I loved every bite. Downside is that I loved it too much, and there is no Surinamese food in SF smh.

Ok, so Suraj on a trip. Running, food, and what else. I don't know if you like it as much, but I love going to museums in new places. Especially history related ones. Not sure if you know, but history is my favorite subject. Sometimes they get repetitive but there are always a few standout pieces in every museum which make it worth for me. If you don't care too much about them, I'll go on my own in the morning, but join me for a couple atleast please :') You should have seen me, Archit, and I think Daniel? in Osaka castle. Everyone else was out in like 30 minutes, but we were there for an hour thirty reading about the history of the castle and Nobunaga and Totoya I think his name was. Riveting history and it was really fun piecing it together with the boys. Archit is fun to talk to about it especially. I also like sightseeing a lot and hiking if that is popular at the destination. Be it going to the top of the tower in a metropolitan city or a bench along the Mediterranean coast, if it is beautiful I want to see it. Well, second to your beauty of course. :) We have to sit there for a little while and take it all in. Holding hands is necessary, and I won't say no if you want to lay your head on my bony shoulder :) . Also, it has always been just a random aspiration of mine to do certain night time activities in every country I visit... so late start some days? If you're down ofc, IUDthi. This is just high level of things I like to do in trips, but honestly I like going where the wind takes me, it's always dependent on the location I go to. You know me, if it's fun I'll do it. 

I get so excited at the thought of adventuring with you Deepthi. You said we are getting older, and that we probably won't have as many adventures. I disagree. Life is an adventure, every aspect of it. Yes, you are probably talking about the bigger things like traveling and seeing other countries, but don't forget the small things. I'm not even talking about hiking or running or biking, but even smaller. Going to Costco is a fun adventure. Shopping for clothes is an adventure. Watching Single's Inferno is definitely a mental adventure. Not sure how many times we want to go on that one. Raising a family is an adventure. Probably the most difficult to be honest. But I know it will be an amazing journey, and I want you there for all of it, Deepthi. Let's explore life together, love.
questions: What was your favorite hike together?
What travel destination fits our vibe the best?
What are your date availabilities for sky diving? I'm booking it for us

days.message:
I love the way you smile. I love the way you make me smile. When I first met you, that was the physical characteristic which kept you apart from others. It’s so genuine and full of kindness. It starts with your wide grin which showcase your incredibly straight, white teeth. I always thought you had really nice teeth, not sure if I had mentioned this before. Like compared to a lot of people, yours is definitely like 95th percentile for sure. Apart from your teeth, you smile so beautifully with your eyes. Your frog shaped eyes. It’s a compliment I swear. The bottom eye lid is flat, and the top has a wide curve (multiple wide curves if you bring up all 35 eyelids) almost like a perfect semi circle. And to top it all off, what warms my heart the most, is the head tilt. Oh, the head tilt. The iconic Deepthi head tilt. The indicator of her getting married soon (Soon better be in 3 years). The way you lean in and get closer when you smile, feels so intimate and wholesome. One of my favorite memories of your smile was actually after Bopi’s housewarming party/birthday? The one where we swapped earrings. But anyways, we went to the club afterwards and I went to the bathroom when we entered. I think I was in line with some friend, but I distinctly remember you tapping me on the shoulder. I turned around, and there was no one there? And then you pop out of the side, smiling, hands behind your back leaning into me, saying "Surprise!" I had been thinking of you the entire night, and I was so thrilled to see you in that moment. Your presence, your face, your wide grin, your head tilt. It all just really made me so happy Deepthi. I remember letting out an extremely wide grin as we chatted in the bathroom line. 

That’s just one of your smiles, the one that most people see. It is genuine, but I feel bad for people that don’t get to experience your other smiles. My favorite smile of yours, is actually the one where I don’t see your beautiful teeth. And you don’t have wide frog eyes in them either. It’s the smile you show when you are in love. It’s a gentle smile, not particularly wide. Your eyes are not wide, they are actually slightly squinted, as if you are really trying to focus on what you are seeing. Head is still tilted, but more like 20 degrees rather than 45. It’s the smile you make only when looking deep into someone’s eyes, so a normal photo would not capture it. I hope on your wedding day, it can be captured because it would make the most phenomenal picture ever. I’m literally smiling so dearly right now, just thinking about that smile. I’ve been smiling this entire chapter. And to top it off, the beauty of this smile is that it’s not just your face, it’s your hand too. Your hand caressing my cheek gently, and slightly pulls as if you want me to come closer. That is. One of the best. Feelings. Ever.

I really wish I took a photo of you that day in Lafayette Park. Your smile was perfect that day, and the backdrop of the park with a sunsetting beams flowing over your soft, flawless skin. Perfection. That would have been my picture for this chapter had I taken it. One of my biggest regrets in life is not taking it. But we will get to Regrets in a later chapter :) 

Now, a little about me. Thank you for loving my smile. I am a generally smiley person, and I think most of my friends know that. Maybe not as much as Kenny, but a personality trait of mine is that I smile a lot. No one ever says I have a good smile however. And I personally don’t like it much either, but I can’t stop because I love being happy and that way I showcase it is by smiling. Also, when I smile, I know it inclines other people to do it as well, either consciously or subconsciously. Like when you are sad, I do my best to smile even more than usual, and look like an idiot even, just so I can get a small smirk out of you. Smiling and saying shitty jokes of course :) I think if we were together, we might need to take breaks from seeing each other, because we would be smiling too much. You would be doing your sucking ritual every 30 minutes or so. I think you might just be toooo happy :)

This chapter was originally titled "Joy", but I am just so in love with your smile that it changed to "Smile". I’ll talk about your laughs in the actual Joy chapter, unless it just turns into one called "Laugh". I hope you never change your smile, Deepthi. Keep all your various smiles. I didn’t even go into your troll smile or confused smile, or the mischievous smile you make when you say Whattttttttt. I get excited just thinking about all your smiling monikers. I love you, Smilethi :)
questions: What is your most fun memory with me?
How high do you value joy in your relationship qualities?
Is it a major concern of yours that you will get laugh lines if you date me?

days.message:
I love how passionate you are. Sometimes I mix up the word dedicated vs passionate, but for you, I think it is pretty much synonymous. You are very dedicated to the things you are passionate about. And if you are not passionate about it, it becomes much harder for you to be dedicated. Let’s start out with why I love your passionate side. Also, this will be mostly about you being passionate rather than all of your passions.

When you are passionate, you really commit to it. The first time I noticed it was actually in a friendship context. You are really passionate about your friends. I know in the past, you mentioned that you lost friends because you didn’t prioritize them or you just found it difficult to keep in touch with a lot of them, but now I don’t see you as anything but caring and passionate about your friends. Let’s start with the simple fact that when a friend asks you about something, you go all out. Do you remember all the times I would text you, and you would send me paragraph responses explaining your stance on something in detail? "Hey Deepthi, should I get a nanoleaf?" "Yes you should, if you want it for these X reasons." "Hey Deepthi, what should we do for our hangout today?" You proceed to list out 15 different options, and then make a whole itinerary of taco spots in the Mission. No matter what I ask, you are always so detailed and caring it astounds me. I don’t want to go too deep into friendship right now since we will talk about it later, but the fact that not being close to Aman brought you to tears, really showcases that passion, Deepthi.

You get really passionate about your hobbies as well. The one I noticed it the most in was not running, but dancing actually. The first time I took notice was when we went to dance class for a Poe class, and you stayed for a second one? I remember we were tired after the first class, and mentally exhausted as well, so Danilo and I just wanted to dip. However, you were trying to convince us to stay for the next class because it was KPOP, and going to be a lot of fun. We didn’t and I expected you to just follow us and leave as well. You didn’t. You stayed to do your dance class and no friend leaving was going to change that. You really enjoy dancing and you are incredibly passionate about it. Side note, I was sad you didn’t come back with us that day, because I looked forward to walks back with you. Sometimes I would suggest walking instead of bus, because it meant I could spend more time with you. Sorry, I got distracted. Anyways… you still are really passionate about dancing still, and I can tell by all the Kpop classes you go to. Sometimes I wonder if you like dancing as a whole, or just KPOP dances. Going all the way to Manhattan and Flushing, from a distant Sacramento, just to do 1/4 of a song. I respect the hustle, Deepthi.

I think for me, the closest thing I have to passion is running. That is the one thing that I can always rely on to keep me sane and provide me endless fun as well. If I could turn that into a career I would :))) Also, thanks for inspiring me to run again :) You saved my sanity. I think passions are very important for mental health, and people should have at least one. I’m honestly very impressed that you have so many. Passionate dancer, runner, friend, foodie, lover… So passionate, my Deepthi. I would go more in depth about your foodieness, but maybe I’ll save it for a later chapter. There is so much more in depth I can go into every single thing if I had the time :((( Never enough time talking or typing.

Stay passionate, Deepthi. I love you.
questions: What do you wish you were more passionate about?
Do you think I am a passionate person?
So do you like the picture?

days.message:
I love how we inspire each other. I thought this would be a great next topic right after we talked about passion, because it is your passions which inspire me to be better. I owe a lot to you, Deepthi. Seeing you excel in what you do, and having fun in your ventures, always makes me want to do them too. They make me want to be better. I think a prime example of this is Volo actually. Didn't think I would bring that up did you. Well this is really important to me, because it was one of the first times I noticed your activities having a direct impact on me. I'm unsure exactly when it was, maybe early 2024, but you did your whole VOLO soccer stint. You just wanted a fun, social activity to do, and you went out and tried VOLO with some friends. I guess it led to some interesting dynamics with Tactical, but regardless it seemed really fun, Deepthi. You would tell us about how you guys would even practice additional days in the park, and I thought it was honestly really attractive how you put yourself out there to try something new. It made me want to do it too. I like soccer, why am I not doing this too. I like sports, and I used to play soccer growing up, so why don't I also sign up. Moving to SF, one of my goals was to try new things, explore, and just have fun figuring out my hobbies and who I am. I was barely doing that my first year, and just partying every weekend to be honest. Seeing you do what I aspired to do in the past, really affected me. Like, this switched my mindset a lot back then into a more explorative mode. I remember you even inviting me to join your league for this. Either your first season, or second. It could have been me just saying how I want to try it and you saying that there is space, or you just asking me to join you. I think you may have asked all three of us, but minor details, you asked me :) I wanted to join your team. One, because I wanted to play soccer since it had been a while. Two, because this was an opportunity to make new friends. Three, because you were there. Oh, how I so wanted to play with you. I'm like a person who enjoys doing physical activities with people to bond. That's my comfort area. But anyways, I said no to you at the time, because I was worried. I was worried to play with you right after "distance" and maybe catch feels for you again. Maybe it was the right choice, maybe it didn't matter too much, but decided against it anyways. However, that didn't stop me. If anything, I thought this was maybe an opportunity to make new friends on my own in my own Volo team, and not latch onto you if I joined yours. And I was happy with my choice. Also, I do like volleyball more than soccer, so I was happy to do that as well :) Thank you for doing Volo, Deepthi. Seeing you pursue new ventures and passions, always inspires me to do so as well. I also got better at volleyball from this, made a couple new friends, and powered my volleyball career for the following year. It gave me something really fun and exciting to look forward to seeing every week, other than you of course :) Also, as a side note. Wow, I think I use also, a lot but I'm not re-reading so I don't know exactly how many times. But anyways, this mentality shift also made me start pursuing more activities in SF, or my life in general. You already know this one very well, it was singing. I started taking this "have fun" mindset and would just do whatever I felt like pursuing. Singing was something I have loved since a child, but never tried to improve on practice. Well, nothing was stopping me now, so might as well try it. And I did :) And I loved it :)

As far as how I inspire you, I really only have a few examples. You maybe have more and didn't tell me, but let me share a few which have been shared. Sometimes when I get interested in something, you also get invested. Maybe, it is because I tell you everything about what I do and draw you in because it is fun, or maybe you like indulging in my interests, unsure. I noticed this for singing. Soon after I signed up for my classes, we would discuss singing a lot. Whenever I saw you, we would talk all about my singing classes, and new techniques I was learning. I would ask you for help understanding terms, and I think I even asked you to be my music theory teacher and teach me what treble clef meant or whatever. "Down," you said. You didn't actually become my teacher but we continued talking about it. You even started singing a bit more, and told me how me singing makes you want to get back into it as well. Something I was really happy about was when me, you, Aman, and Rish went to get Canes. We were talking about singing, and I brought up how you used to sing in elementary school. Rish looked at you and was like "What? I didn't know you sang?" I loved that comment. Woah, Deepthi shared something with me that even Rish doesn't know about. I felt so close to you in that moment, like we had our own hobby, that just me and you share. Also, I really appreciate how you still haven't let go of the singing thing, and we still talk about things like key changes and my vocal lessons to this day. Let's never lose that. An additional benefit of you being interested in my hobbies, is that it's like a power up. Makes me commit to them more knowing that you are rooting for me :)

Something funny, was the fact that you get inspired to do things to impress me :) Like when you are coming back to the bay, you purposefully run more or watch your diet because you have to look your best around me. You look perfect by the way, regardless, so don't starve yourself. Actually who am I kidding, Foodthi never starves herself in NY. Sometimes you go on runs because you know I am as well, and you want to keep up. You practice more so that the next time we meet up, you will be ready for our long run. You take videos of your dance classes, so that I can see them :) You started reading the cook book more and want to be a better cook, because it was fun to talk to me about when I started my culinary adventure. Thanks for not shitting on my negative breaded chicken too much. You are really cute, Deepthi. Let's keep inspiring each other to be better. Also, there will be a whole chapter on growth, which will go beyond hobbies, so stay tuned. I love you.
questions: Do you think I'm giving you too much credit for inspiring me?
Would you say more of your ventures come from other people inspiring you, or is it self-motivated?
Why have we still not duetted a song together???

days.message:
I get scared. I'm sorry, I couldn't think of a way to start this sentence with I love. I don't love the fact that I get scared. I don't like being fearful. But, it is just a part of who I am and I have to accept it. It's like 11 PM right now, and I just messaged you Insta and wished you a goodnight. I'm really scared. I'm scared that June 15th is approaching. I'm scared that you will think I don't care about you because I am not coming to Philly. I'm scared that this is destroying you mentally and probably physically as well. I'm scared that I will lose you forever.

I wouldn't say I have always been a fearful person. Other than spiders, The Grudge, and maybe the occasional cockroach (yes, I was scared of them too when I was younger), nothing really scared me. Although I slept with the light on until I was 11, I wasn't really that scared of the dark like some kids. Maybe the most fear I felt was when my parents confronted me. Grades scared me. The amount of fear I would get when I had to show my parents that I got anything less than an A, would send shivers down my spine. I would start sweating and panic on how to lie to them, or hide the report card. I remember even turning off the computer when they would login to the computer to check my grades, and blame the computer for being faulty. My mom caught me press the power button once, and afterwards I had to stand behind her when she checked. I wasn't very cunning as a child. This is probably why I got grey hairs at 16. All the stress from school really got to me. I can still feel the anxiety from those days, and how my stomach would churn when my mom asked me for my grades. I hated that feeling and was so relieved when it went away in college. I don't think I ever really felt stress/fear to that degree after high school.

When I moved to SF, it was still the same in the beginning. First few months, it was all just excitement and fun. Zero worries. I wasn't scared of anything. Everything was perfect. I suppose I was afraid that I wouldn't find a girlfriend in March/April 2023, but it wasn't a huge worry. Then came June 2023, when I asked you out. I wouldn't say I really felt fear or anxiety from the situation; it was mostly just sadness at that time. However, fear started building up after that. By the time we went to LA together, I started to get more feelings for you, and wanted to get closer to you even if I didn't convey it to you explicitly. I was still holding on to you, and was hoping that there would be another opportunity to date you, to the point where I would just be thinking of you for days on end. I always wanted to see you and would run to your place hoping to maybe meet you for like 5 minutes. I really wanted to date you. So much, that just thinking of dating you would make me smile. But this was why I became so fearful. I was so invested in you, that I would overthink my actions around you and worry that they might go wrong. For example, I really wanted to ask you for casual in LA, but I was too afraid of "giving you the ick" and losing the friendship. And after we came back from LA, I had your location and I would look at it ALL the time. I would see you at Rish's place and get worried. "Why is she staying at his place overnight? Are they just friends? Is she dating him?" I was terrified that I would lose you before the new year. The fear I felt then was reminiscent of my childhood days, except this time I cried. I hadn't cried for atleast 10 years prior. I was anxious, scared, confused, and didn't know what to think. And when I asked you out the second time, I still held onto hope even though you said no, and thought eventually things could still work out between us. I don't know if you remember my journal, but that's what I wrote in there back then. I was still fearful however, because I would see how close you and Rish were. Even on your birthday, when we surprised you day of in 2023, I was worried when Rish didn't come back home with us and stayed longer. I knew something was happening there but I didn't want to believe it. But, I was scared, really scared. I could see the end in sight.

Fast forward to 2025, I didn't really feel much fear the prior year. Maybe some times I would for general things like spiders/feet, but friends were solid, I went on some dates, running was improving, nothing to complain about. I felt very close to you, and at that time in like Jan 2025, I don't think I saw you as anything more than a friend. My friend Deepthi, who I would be excited to see every few days. I guess I should have known from my excitement back then of how I felt. It felt fun. No Rish, just fun Deepthi, and we would laugh and smile all the time. But by May 2025, fear starting setting in. I would be losing Deepthi in a few months, and she will be gone. Part of my life, a very important part of my life, will be gone. The amount of fun and growth I had with you didn't compare with anyone else, and I was so scared of losing you and essentially losing a part of SF as well. It wouldn't be the same without you. I needed Deepthi. This fear in me, was probably how I knew I loved you. And for that one reason, I'm happy I have fear. Those feelings of my stomach churning, cold sweats, and light-headedness, guided me to the biggest revelation of my life. I love this girl, I love Deepthi. I didn't want you to leave, I needed you to stay, but I was too late. That day outside your place when I confessed, my heart was pounding so fast, and other than love and maybe sadness, the biggest emotion was fear. She's leaving in a week, and I love her. Once she's gone, I can never be with her. I lost her. I thought this would be the end.

Now, I know what fear is. Fear is this feeling that I have right now. Fear for me isn't related to anything tangible, fear is the worry of losing something that you can't get back. My fear is losing Deepthi. My fear is living my life without her by my side. I have never been more scared of anything in my whole life. Not everyone is lucky enough to find their fated soulmate in life, but I was somehow fortunate enough to find mine, and I am scared of losing her. I love you, Deepthi.

Did your feelings for me ever scare you?
Why are you so deathly afraid of cockroaches?? They are kinda cute sometimes.

days.message:
I loved my childhood. I think a person's upbringing defines who they are at a very early age. Yes, you go off to college, move away, meet new people, gather new experiences, but there will always be certain characteristics which will remain with you for your whole life. Before we went into more topics, I wanted to take a step back and have you learn a little bit more about me and where I come from. I feel like I tell you a lot about what I was like at a child, but as you know I was a very weird, complex child, and there will always be a new story I could tell you every week. So let's start with some that shaped me into the man you met and fell in love with. Yes, some of them will be cringe, but dude... I have normal experiences too.

I grew up pretty superstitious about things. My parents are religious, my mom mostly, and it led to them imposing restrictions on things that I can/cannot do which have stuck around me to this day. I assume you may have heard some of these, or maybe have had these rules in your own household as well. The first one was that since God was in everything, I wasn't allowed to touch anything with my feet, especially books. It comes from some old story about Narasimha (I bet you don't know who that is), where there was some man breaking a bunch of random objects for fun because he believed that God doesn't exist inside objects, but then Narasimha came out and killed(?) the man proving that he was inside. Yeah idk bro. But anyways, because of that I don't like touching objects with my feet, and sometimes even people. My mom would yell at me if I ever pushed my sister with my feet, and would say I need to mokku her. Is that a Telugu word idk, but I would have to like touch her and touch my eyes in forgiveness. Also, don't turn this into some weird root cause of my foot aversion, that was very different. And if you know anything about me now, I'm okay with feet touching other people, and certain feet touching me as well. Moving on. Another thing was, after a haircut, I wasn't allowed to touch ANYTHING. Like literally my mom would tell me and my sister that until we took a shower, we can't even poke something. It was so extreme that when we got in the car to go back home, I wouldn't touch my seatbelt with my hand, and my mom would come to the backseat and put it on for me. Yes, it was that serious apparently. I used to think that it was because in Indian culture I am "unclean" and touching things like spread negative energy or something. Idk, I believed anything. And because of that, I still try not to touch things after I get a haircut, and I HAVE to shower otherwise I will just feel gross the entire day. Like physically gross, and spiritually??? But, I am a lot more relaxed about it now. I literally touch a steering wheel after my haircuts now, how I have grown. But get this, there's more to this story. I talked to my mom today (it's 6/9 now), and asked her about this haircut superstition and why it was a thing. I never asked when I was younger, and blindly followed, but now after 29 years of wisdom, I decided to get an answer. She literally told me that it started because in the olden days people in India would get their haircuts from Sudra's (which are a lower caste), and if you are touched by them you become dirty. It's not necessarily because they were like physically dirty, but because they were from a lower class and their presence was filthy. So basically, I was a racist child, and followed this rule because I believed in the caste system, apparently. Great. But then, my mom saved it by telling me that she doesn't believe in that, and she just told us not to touch things so our little hairs wouldn't get everywhere. I guess she was just really trying to sell the superstition thing by even buckling in my seatbelt for me. I believed anything as a child. So long story short, I need to touch everything around me after a haircut now, to make up for my possible caste-ism as a young child. Maybe I need to question more of my childhood experiences, and see if it is something I need to still follow. I have a lot of random quirks about me Deepthi (unlike Deku), and you may be confused at first, but you will grow to love how unique I am :) Like, don't get me started about what my mom taught me about not wasting food...

My parents gave me more than just superstitions, they gave me a lot of love too which I still carry today. Although my parents yelled, fought, cried in arguments, there was an equal amount of love. Also it was mostly my dad causing the arguments, typical brown dad smh. I would NEVER want to be like that, and hurt my family in any way. Communication isn't their strong suit. Sorry, I digress. Anyways, there was a lot of love. Whenever my mom dropped me off at school, she would give me a kiss and tell me she loves me. Every single day until like middle school, unless I was late and just ran out to get to class. I would say it back begrudgingly, because I thought it was uncool to say that to your mom, and if my friends saw it, they would bully me for the rest of elementary. Yeah, I couldn't ruin my elementary rep. Archit and Abhi would have never let it go, even to this day of course. But, even though I took it for granted, those small actions made me feel loved and happy. It made me feel safe. It's a feeling I haven't forgotten for one second. I think I only really started saying it back after I left home. Being apart from my family, made me really miss them and love them. So everytime we call and say bye, it doesn't end unless I say I love you back to my mom. It is the norm now. Went from me never saying it, to my mom calling me back if she didn't hear me say I love you at the end of a call. It made me REALLY happy when she did that. I love my mom so much, and I love how she made me a more empathetic and loving person. That's also why I felt pretty emotional when you told me you miss your mom. How you felt really sad this time saying bye. How she fell down as you were leaving. My heart was in woe when you typed that. I can just imagine how I would feel if that happened in front of me. Mothers are amazing. I know you really don't say it, but I wish you would tell your mom you love her. I guess I say "love" a lot nowadays to you, but it really means a lot to me, Deepthi. That word has so much power everytime. 

My childhood shaped into the weird, wacky guy you know today, for better or for worse. I have a lot of interesting childhood memories and I'm sure you will be hearing a lot more of them in the future. I also want to hear more about yours, and what you were like growing up. How your parents influenced your life, your brother, your friends, and the random things you do today that were ingrained in you from childhood. I want to hear all of it, Deepthi, so please tell me sometime. And in true Suraj fashion, I can't end without saying it. I love you.

Did your parents ever make you follow any supersitions?
Sorry, this isn't even a question. I just want to say that I believe you will be an amazing, loving mother.
Honestly, do you think I'm courageous?
Do you think your courage has increased or decreased since you moved to NY?
So do we keep the audio only format, or should I go back?

What is your password???

days.message:
Hi. I don't even know what to type about today. I feel like my mind is still processing all the emotions I felt today. I just moved into my new room. It gave me something to think about instead of you. At least I tried to for just a few hours. I don't think I've smiled at all in the past 6 hours. Thomas and Kasey showed up and started helping me move things. They instantly started re-organizing and throwing out old things. They even rotated the entire living room like they said they would and made the couch longer than it was before. I don't like it. It feels more cramped and I don't want this change. The one thing they allowed me to have in the room was an area to roll out after a run. And then, they are going to add another table under the white board but it's already very packed with more big furniture to come. Maybe I'm just emotional and want to overreact. Have undeserving rage towards my new roommates who are just trying to make their new place look as clean and feng shui as possible. It's crazy how different it looks already. And my new room has everything set up now, and all I need to do now and organize my clothes. It was such a tedious and long process disassembling the bed by myself and moving it over and reassembling. I think I cut myself 4 times and gave myself a couple bruises in the process. I couldn't even get myself to eat, because I knew that if I took a break I would start crying. That's literally all I wanted to do. Cry. If I didn't have anything else to do, I would just sit in my room on 600 calories and cry while thinking of what I could do to be with you. What I could do to convince you that it is worth leaving your perfect relationship. What do I have over your perfect relationship? Telling you that I understand you better and will help you reach your baseline happiness? Stating that your happiest moments are always around me (or so you tell me)? Ask you to listen to those feelings that keep you from committing to Rish? I'm lost on what I can do to convince you. You say you aren't ready to make the decision now, but I know that just means that you pick Rish. If you aren't leaving him, that just means you will stay with him and continue with life as it is. I wish, I really wish, you could take time away from both of us and decide how you feel. No other influences, and just your feelings/thoughts. Logical ones, emotional ones, scary ones. Experience them all yourself before deciding. You have an obligation to Rish, sure. You say he is like family, sure. But, you also have an obligation to yourself to decide what you want for yourself for the rest of your life, so I plead you to take that time.

How many times will my heart feels like it's breaking? How many time can I take this feeling? I'm surprised that I'm like at least 50% mentally stable still, because I doubt others would in my situation. Most people would have given up by now if it was me. Many people probably would have dropped it the moment you went to NY. Another bunch would have dropped after you went on a birthday trip with your boyfriends and friends in Paris. Others would have probably dropped after they got a girlfriend in November. The ones who stuck around might have left after December, when the future seemed questionable still and they started forming stronger feelings for their girlfriend. And I think 99% would have been gone after March when the love of their life said they choose not you. I stayed. The crazy in love, future with Deepthi seeking man stayed until right now. He never stopped seeing a future with Deepthi, and still sees it after all that was said. And he doesn't plan to stop. Like I said, my love is tenacious and it's unrelenting. This is why I say I won't lose feelings for you ever, and I won't let you either. You have tried to lose interest in me multiple times over the past year, but I keep pulling you back in for better or worse. That's how I know you will never lose your passion with me. It's also why I can't be friends with you after all this. It's either be your partner or nothing at all. I'm sorry if this has sounded aggressive or unsettling, but my love for you is incredibly strong, Deepthi, and I don't think I can ever move on after you.

And now, I'm in one of my depresso episodes. The ones where I don't feel like doing anything, and Becca keeps messaging me to check if I am okay. She literally said "I like you alive", Suraj. Bruh. Everyone thinks it is because I'm switching to a new room, and getting a new roommate. That's part of it for sure, but that is miniscule compared to all the emotions. I'm surprised Thomas and Kasey didn't comment on my demeanor at all to be honest. I guess they don't know me that well or thought I was tired. I talked to Pooja as well after they left. She had a really shitty shift yesterday night, but I didn't even feel like asking her anything about it. I just feel guilty everytime I see her now, knowing that I'm trying my absolute best to not be with her. Between me and you Deepthi, I'm 100% the shittier person. I'm the one trying to destroy what everyone has so that I can be with the girl I love. The worst part is that I feel some guilt when I see Pooja, but otherwise I don't feel ashamed about anything. I'm convinced that being with you is worth all this pain and emotional turmoil. You are worth it. I want to risk everything for you, because if I lose you, I don't know if I'll find anyone else who will understand me like you do. Ignoring all the deceit, you make me a better person. Yes, that is a lot to ignore. Sometimes, I wonder if that tarnishes your image of me, and it makes me a less ideal person to date. Even now, after ranting about my feelings in this chapter, I wonder if it makes you like me less and see me as a crazy person. Also, side note, I'm thinking about telling Pooja about the feelings I still harbor for you this weekend. I think it's time to tell her. She literally kept asking me what was wrong, and I had no answer, so I'll probably just tell her. Maybe being honest will make me be a better person idk. I also wonder if you will still continue reading these. I'm sorry for this chapter, it's just a mess of thoughts. I hate myself :( , but I love you Deepthi. That won't change. If you are still reading, maybe expect better content on a more sane day.

I still hope you pick me, Deepthi. I haven't given up. But if you tell me, "Suraj, I don't want to be with you, and I will never be with you", I'll leave you forever and you won't have to stress anymore.
questions: Do you think I'm a creep?
In your heart, do you still think there is something I could do to convince you?
Would you accept the platinum ring if you get it via my will one day? Just jokes btw :)

days.message:
I hate logic sometimes. People take those MBTI tests and wonder if they are Thinking or Feeling. I always thought of myself as the Thinking guy, since I like playing strategy games and I'm a computer scientist, and I like reasoning things. I do all those things, but I think with so much emotion sometimes that I wonder if I am a Feeling person. Right now, that is definitely what I am, I'm thinking 80% emotion, and 20% logic. And it seems for you, you are thinking of things the opposite way. Maybe back in July/August it was emotion, but now it is almost all logic. I hate that but I understand it. You already tried the emotional route, and it didn't work out for you. And now with your emotions not at the peak that it was once at, logic overwhelms it. I still make you smile and laugh, and show you that you are cared for, but that isn't enough anymore. Logically, it doesn't make sense to you. I still believe if you were more passionate about being with me, you could make it happen. If you had more emotion, you could make the leap. And that just makes me think, that I just didn't try hard enough to be with you. I didn't want it enough. I prioritized my other life activities over you, and didn't come to Philly. I didn't show you how much I am willing to do for you. I made you take the logic route, because my emotions weren't enough, and nothing you tell me will convince me otherwise. Also, to clarify, logical doesn't mean outright going with Rish. It's evaluating what makes logical sense for your future. Caring for emotions is also logical, it's something you require for your future. Having a healthy household in the future is also a consideration, since that is important for you children. Logic still involves thinking about emotions, and what they mean for your future holistically.

Also Deepthi, I lied. I lied at the end of our call yesterday (6/12) because I was scared of how my answer would affect you. But, I will tell you the truth now, as your friend, and not the man who begs to be with you. If I was your friend, removed from the situation, but somehow knew every single detail, I would tell you this. If you are happy with your boyfriend and view it as the perfect relationship, you should focus on him and what you guys have. Suraj seems great as well, but there are too many unknowns with him and if you have something strong already why would you give it up. It doesn't make sense. You ended things with Suraj in March, and you said it was the happiest you have been in a long while, and also since then, things with Rish has improved. He fixed all the things that you were worried about, and has shown you that you guys can conquer anything together. Deepthi, you are going to weddings with him, have lived with him for a year now, and he's going to go stay with your parents soon. You are already doing so well, do you really want to give it up? Is there something with Suraj that transcends all of this, that makes the change worth? If there is something more that I don't see, go for it I guess, but on paper sounds like you would regret it. Also, Suraj seems really emotional, and who knows if that is something you need in your life. Maybe you really do need someone different than you to keep you in check. Also, I see your passion for Suraj has declined, and you are also not letting him go because he fights so hard. I'm not saying you need to just decide to get engaged to Rish right now, but if that's the one you value more, you should try to make it work without Suraj in the picture. If you really think that it is too hard to decide right now, and you don't want to let Suraj go, maybe you need these two months to decide. Don't worry about the logistics, this is for your future. Make a decision you are proud of and don't look back. I can't tell you what to do, but think about your future and who you want to spend it with. Think with both logic and emotion. A relationship isn't just one or the other. Both of these guys are amazing I can tell, but also keep in mind that you may be losing one of them forever depending on the route you take. It's tough love, but you just have to accept it and move on.

That is what I would tell you. It hurt to type all that out, but I love you, Deepthi. I respect you and I have to be honest with you, even if it hurts me in the end.
questions: Do you wish I was less emotional?
How much are you valuing your emotions in your decision?
Logically, does it make sense for you to ult in as Kai'sa, 15 seconds after you call for it?
